metadata.curation_note: Promoted after arXiv abstract review; arXiv page lists PRL 2026 journal reference.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: quantum geometry
nodes.summary: Band-wavefunction geometry, including Berry curvature and quantum metric, that can tune stability of fractional Chern and competing crystalline phases.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: multiband correlations
nodes.summary: Correlations involving higher moire bands that can renormalize or stabilize fractional Chern states beyond one-band exact diagonalization.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: transient optical spectroscopy
nodes.summary: Nonequilibrium optical probe that can reveal fractional states and dynamics not visible in static sensing or transport.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: pump-probe circular dichroism
nodes.summary: Time-resolved magnetic/valley optical probe used to infer hidden or weak magnetic signatures in twisted MoTe2.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: chiral superconductivity
nodes.summary: Superconducting phase with broken time-reversal/chiral topology, proposed near anomalous Hall and fractional Chern phases in twisted MoTe2.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: fractional topological insulator candidate
nodes.summary: Time-reversal-related fractional topological phase candidate, currently requiring careful distinction between magnetic signatures and helical-edge transport confirmation.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: topological electronic crystal
nodes.summary: Competing correlated topological crystalline phase reported as a remote-layer-tuned alternative to FQAH/CFL behavior in moire MoTe2.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: MCD and trion PL support zero-field FQAH states in 3.7 degree twisted MoTe2 at nu=-2/3 and -3/5.
nodes.summary: The arXiv/Nature report states that MCD reveals robust ferromagnetic fractional states and trion PL Landau-fan shifts at nu=-2/3 and -3/5 matching Streda dispersions for fractionally quantized Hall conductance; nu=-1 behaves as a C=-1 QAH state.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: 2.1 degree twisted MoTe2 shows transport evidence for a fractional QSH insulator at nu=3.
nodes.summary: The arXiv report describes a fractional QSH state at nu=3 with zero anomalous Hall response and edge conductance 3/2 G0 per edge, alongside integer QSH states at nu=2, 4, and 6.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: The reported nu=3 FQSH regime can show low-field time-reversal-symmetry breaking despite zero Hall response above about 20 mT.
nodes.summary: The 2025 arXiv report frames the nu=3 state as a phase with zero Hall resistance above roughly 20 mT but finite anomalous Hall response and hysteresis at very low fields, implying spontaneous time-reversal-symmetry breaking in part of the FQSH phenomenology.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: Transient optical spectroscopy can reveal many hidden fractional states in tMoTe2 that static optical sensing or transport misses.
nodes.summary: The arXiv report says pump-probe optical spectroscopy reveals nearly 20 hidden fractional states between nu=-1 and -3, including candidate non-Abelian and time-reversal-symmetric topological states that are absent in static optical sensing or transport.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: Hidden tMoTe2 fractional states can have distinct fast and slow melting/recovery timescales.
nodes.summary: The hidden-state arXiv report identifies fast 2-4 ps electronic thermalization and slower 180-270 ps phonon heating timescales in the recovery dynamics.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: Twisted MoTe2 superconductivity near an anomalous Hall metal and FCI regime is argued to be chiral.
nodes.summary: The 2025 arXiv/PRL report argues that spin/valley polarization, anomalous Hall hysteresis, adjacency to a fractional Chern insulator, and a narrow zero-gate-field dome together indicate chiral superconductivity.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: A Kohn-Luttinger/RPA treatment finds dominant p+ip intravalley pairing at zero gate field in twisted MoTe2.
nodes.summary: The theory identifies a dominant intravalley p+ip pairing dome at zero gate field, contrasting with weaker time-reversal-symmetric intervalley pairing at finite gate field.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: Higher moire bands can materially change FCI stability in twisted MoTe2.
nodes.summary: The DSRG arXiv report argues that higher-band effects cannot be ignored: at nu=1/3 they reduce the FCI gap, while at nu=2/3 dynamic multiband correlations can stabilize the FCI at larger twist angles.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: A remote layer can tune moire MoTe2 from FQAH/CFL behavior toward topological electronic crystals.
nodes.summary: The 2025 arXiv report compares A-A and A-AB trilayer regions and says the remote B layer changes quantum geometry before charge transfer, replacing A-A CFL/FQAH behavior with topological electronic crystal and quantum anomalous Hall crystal states at selected fractional fillings.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: A putative FTI signature in twisted MoTe2 is reported as an Ising-antiferromagnetic nu=-4/3 state with millitesla first-order transitions.
nodes.summary: The 2026 arXiv report uses pump-probe circular dichroism to identify a nu=-4/3 state at 3.7 and 3.9 degrees with no net magnetization and first-order transitions around 2-6 mT to partially valley-polarized states, while noting absence in 4.0 and 3.3 degree samples.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: Does the putative nu=-4/3 FTI show helical edge quantization or direct spatial structure?
nodes.summary: The magnetic signature is suggestive but not equivalent to direct helical-edge transport or imaging confirmation; future interpretation should keep the state labeled putative unless stronger evidence is available.

provenance.method: Codex curation inference from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: PL and RMCD optically detect a fragile nu=-1/3 FQAH state in twisted MoTe2.
nodes.summary: The 2026 arXiv/PRL report says PL and RMCD identify ferromagnetic states at nu=-1, -2/3, and -1/3, with Curie temperatures about 11 K, 3.5 K, and 2.4 K; the nu=-1/3 state has a narrower electric-field range and lower temperature scale.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: The nu=-1/3 PL spectrum disperses with out-of-plane magnetic field, supporting a nontrivial topological interpretation.
nodes.summary: The optical -1/3 FQAH report states that PL spectra at nu=-1/3 disperse with increasing out-of-plane magnetic field and that exact-diagonalization calculations support a topologically nontrivial interpretation.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: D93 Run2 deck reports a near-nu=-2 RMCD magnetic/saturation feature that strengthens with displacement field but lacks clear hysteresis.
nodes.summary: Slides 18-22 of the D93 Run2 deck report a possible magnetic feature near nu=-2 and n=-9.8, increasing distinctness with D field and saturation-like diagonal structure, while still noting no clear hysteresis at nu=-2 after signal optimization.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass over extracted deck text
original deck path

nodes.label: Why does D93 show near-nu=-2 magnetic/saturation contrast without robust hysteresis?
nodes.summary: The deck suggests checking wavelength sensitivity, spot selection outside contacts, and closer phase-diagram mapping before interpreting the feature as a robust hysteretic magnetic state.

provenance.method: Codex curation inference from extracted deck text

nodes.label: D6 deck reports a nu=-2/3 fractional optical feature near true D=0 that is strongly displacement-field sensitive.
nodes.summary: Slides 14-16 of the D6 Attodry911 deck report a potential trion fractional state, then a clearer nu=-2/3 state at a new spot; the clearest signal is near D=0 and the cuts show a large transition between positive and negative D.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass over extracted deck text
original deck path

nodes.label: C5 deck treats bilayer trion fractional features and hBN-hole dot staircases as actionable optical signals.
nodes.summary: The C5 deck reports clear bilayer trion emission with visible fractional features, many fractions below -1/2, dot-like spectra in hBN holes, a staircase at B=1.2 T, and dispersion that needs better data near 3.1 degrees.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass over extracted deck text
original deck path
